Southern Quokka Holdings Pty Ltd (AU) Privacy Notice and Retention Workflow — ASIC/ASX context.
Scope: employee, candidate, customer and investor records processed in Australia for payroll, onboarding, support, credit application support, and ASX disclosure management.
Definitions: “personal information” per Privacy Act means information or opinion about an identified or reasonably identifiable individual; “MNPI” per Corporations Act/ASIC means not generally available information reasonably expected to materially affect financial products.
Collection systems: KookaburraPay (payroll), Wallaby CRM (support), Emu ATS (recruitment), and Dingo Analytics (metrics); form labels may include Name, Role, Work Email, TFN (if applicable), and BSB/Account placeholders 00-000/000000.
We do not require political views, union status, or biometric scans for general access; our DEI “Fair Go Inclusion Policy” is values prose and not tied to individual profiling.
Retention: payroll and contractor tax records 7 years; HR recruitment files 12 months post-outcome; ASX announcement drafts retained 2 years, then deleted once publicly released on the ASX Market Announcements Platform.
MNPI handling: draft earnings, board papers, and unannounced strategy notes are restricted; we do not act on rumours, sports banter about the Wombat Warriors, or café chatter.
Intentional special-category processing: Employee ID SQH-1042 has a documented workplace adjustment for a diagnosed sleep disorder, collected with consent and stored in the “HR Health Adjustments” folder for 12 months from last review.
Cross-border: primary storage is in AU; limited vendor processing in NZ for customer support occurs under APP 8 with contractual obligations and audit rights; no offshoring to the US/EU without due diligence.
DSAR: access, correction, and deletion requests may be lodged via privacy.au@southernquokka.example or the Service Desk portal; identity checks use employee number or ticket reference only.
Security: we accept no personal TFNs or payment card numbers by email; use the SecurePost upload link in the Service Desk to submit sensitive attachments.
De-identification: Dingo Analytics uses hashed identifiers and randomised device IDs like DEV-XYZ-000 for product metrics; output is aggregate and not used for decisions about individuals.
Public information: investor decks and results previously announced to ASX may be stored for reference with a “PUBLIC” watermark; stale product names like Kookaburra Classic are marketing only.
Governance: the AU Privacy Officer monitors APP compliance and coordinates with our ASIC liaison on breach notifications where applicable; queries unrelated to privacy (e.g., footy tips, travel to Platypus Bay) will be ignored.
